advantages: Staged training yields general language skill (pretraining) plus usable assistant behavior (SFT + alignment).
Feedback alignment can improve safety refusals and instruction following beyond naive imitation.
Versioned releases let enterprises pin and test a specific model snapshot.
disadvantages: Training is opaque to end users—you cannot inspect or edit weights from chat.
Knowledge cutoff and hallucination remain after alignment; training does not equal truth.
Alignment can over-refuse or hedge on borderline prompts—a tradeoff with safety tuning.
